page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r8y.2xlarge is an arm64-based virtual server instance powered by the Alibaba Yitian YiTian710 processor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 2.75 GHz. The CPU architecture includes 64 KB of L1 data cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 64 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It operates on the KVM hypervisor, does not include local storage, and has a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Benchmark results place the ecs.r8y.2xlarge in the average performance tier for most standard operations. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests yielded scores of 1820.48 and 14486.45 respectively, while the PassMark CPU score reached 6807.57. Memory performance is supported by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 819.63 GB/sec, though uncached large block write bandwidth drops to 35.47 GB/sec. A notable performance bottleneck is observed in cryptographic operations, where the PassMark CPU encryption test scored 1327.33, placing it in the weak category. Additionally, while the instance handles small-scale language model inference adequately, performance drops significantly on medium-sized models, with 7B parameter model prompt processing scoring a poor 10.62 tokens/sec.

Given its arm64 architecture and high memory density, the ecs.r8y.2xlarge offers a cost-efficient profile for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local storage or heavy cryptographic processing. The instance is well-suited for database operations, as demonstrated by its PassMark database score of 8099.29 and Redis SET throughput of 12.61 million operations per second. It is also appropriate for static web serving, which achieved 12.11 GB/sec in testing, as well as memory-optimized caching layers, lightweight data processing, and general-purpose arm64 development.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r8y.2xlarge is an arm64-based, memory-optimized virtual server powered by an 8-core Alibaba Yitian YiTian710 processor running at 2.75 GHz. It features 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio, but includes no local storage and relies on a 5 Gbps baseline network. Performance benchmarks generally fall into the average tier, with a PassMark CPU score of 6807.57 and a Redis SET throughput of 12.61 million operations per second. However, cryptographic performance is a notable bottleneck, scoring in the weak tier on PassMark encryption tests. Large-scale LLM inference is also limited, with poor throughput on 7B parameter models. This instance is best suited for memory-intensive workloads such as database operations, caching layers, static web serving, and general arm64 application hosting where local storage is not required.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r8y.2xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized server with 8 Yitian710 vCPUs and 64 GB RAM. It offers balanced database and web serving performance but exhibits weak cryptographic throughput.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r8y.2xlarge is an arm64 virtual server featuring 8 Yitian710 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 5 Gbps baseline network for memory-intensive tasks.
tagline: An arm64 virtual server featuring high memory density and dedicated CPU cores for database and memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-core Alibaba Yitian710 arm64 processor at 2.75 GHz
Equipped with 64 GB of RAM, offering 8 GB per core memory density
Delivers 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Performs well in database operations and Redis caching workloads
Exhibits weak performance in cryptographic and medium-scale LLM tasks
